assertions.expectation: Explains this is related to the interaction between maxSurge, drain order, and pod scheduling
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains how maxSurge creates surge nodes that aren't ready while nodes are being cordoned, causing scheduling conflicts between drain order and pod placement

assertions.expectation: Notes that GKE cordons nodes before draining them — pods should prefer scheduling onto uncordoned (upgraded) nodes
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'GKE cordons and drains multiple nodes simultaneously before the surge nodes are fully ready' and explains the scheduler sees cordoned nodes as available

assertions.expectation: Identifies possible cause: too many nodes being drained simultaneously (high maxSurge) before enough upgraded nodes exist
assertions.reason: Response identifies 'When maxSurge is set too high' as the root cause and provides specific example showing how multiple nodes get cordoned before surge nodes are ready

assertions.expectation: Recommends reducing maxSurge to 1 to ensure upgraded nodes are available before draining the next batch
assertions.reason: Response provides specific gcloud command to set --max-surge-upgrade 1 and explains this 'creates 1 surge node at a time' and 'waits for it to be Ready before draining the next node'

assertions.expectation: Suggests using pod anti-affinity or node affinity to prefer already-upgraded nodes
assertions.reason: Response provides kubectl patch command with nodeAffinity configuration and mentions 'Missing pod anti-affinity rules' as a contributing factor

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if any taints or tolerations are causing pods to land on the wrong nodes
assertions.reason: Response does not mention taints or tolerations as potential causes for pods landing on wrong nodes

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl commands to check node cordoned status and pod scheduling events
assertions.reason: Response provides multiple kubectl commands including 'kubectl get nodes | grep SchedulingDisabled' to check cordoned status and monitoring commands to watch pod placement

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting maxUnavailable=0 to ensure old nodes aren't removed until pods are safely rescheduled
assertions.reason: Response consistently shows --max-unavailable-upgrade 0 in all gcloud commands and explains this ensures pods are safely rescheduled

assertions.expectation: May mention GKE's autoscaled blue-green upgrade strategy as an alternative that avoids this issue by cordoning the entire old pool before migrating workloads to new nodes
assertions.reason: Response provides detailed section on 'Autoscaled Blue-Green' with specific gcloud command and explains it 'creates the entire green pool first' and 'cordons the blue pool only after green nodes are Ready'
